campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to how much local businesses pay in license taxes. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed tax rate updates, and the council publicly discussed the changes before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted for the upcoming fiscal year
Finance department recommended specific new tax amounts for local businesses
Council members debated the proposed changes in a public meeting before voting
Changes to the Campbell Municipal Code were needed to make the tax updates official

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, they decided to move forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The council directed staff to put it on the consent calendar so it can be formally adopted at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
First reading waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for consent calendar vote at next meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to handle routine administrative business including approving meeting minutes and reviewing the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the town manager to sign off on several construction contracts and agreements for professional services to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes from April 7 closed and public sessions
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other contractors
Approved town improvement projects and infrastructure work

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and discuss several projects affecting the city. Major items included plans to replace an elevator at a terminal building, reviewing bids for cleaning up homeless encampments, updating a digital advertising agreement with Clear Channel, and approving a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade underway
Encampment Cleanup Services - seeking vendors to address homeless encampments
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel - updating terms for large format ads on city property
Light the Night Firefighter Memorial - approving District 7's special event and donations

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments from residents, and review funding requests from neighborhood groups for the 2026-27 budget year. The council discussed which neighborhood grant applications should receive city funding and heard updates on how often residents are attending neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget
Public comments and concerns from residents
Attendance trends at neighborhood meetings
Approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, focused on interviewing candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to watch and comment on the proceedings either in person at City Hall or online.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting to negotiate with the school district about potentially buying or selling a property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The city's manager and attorney discussed the price and payment terms for this deal. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans for getting grant money to fund it in the future.
keyTopics: Negotiation to buy or sell property at 10480 Finch Avenue with the school district
Discussion of price and payment terms for the property deal
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new employment contract with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026
Firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve the city's spending plans for the next five years and discuss the upcoming year's budget. They also approved a master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the council operates.
keyTopics: 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031) approved
FY 2026-27 operating budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council procedures and protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including internal scheduling and leadership elections. The main focus was a public hearing on a proposal to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot on Old Trace Road into 9 single-family homes and 7 smaller accessory units, served by a new private street.
keyTopics: Proposed subdivision of Old Trace Road lot into 9 houses + 7 accessory units
New private street construction to access the residential development
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Council schedule and work assignments
